global_notes: Fast, dense cut — 63 beats in 36.5s averages ~0.58s per shot, which reads as a tightly music-locked edit rather than a breathing instructional piece. Overhead is clearly the workhorse (2:1 over front), almost certainly carrying the prep and technique middle where hands-on-board clarity matters; front angles are reserved for opener/hero moments and the transformation/plating bookends where you want face, pour, or reveal energy. The editor is aggressive against the pipeline — 27 added beats vs only 2 from the editorial group suggests they're subdividing phrases and layering flash cuts on top of the baseline structure to ride the track rather than just landing on its downbeats. That ratio usually signals accelerating stack-ups (montage bursts on prep, quick multi-angle coverage of a single action) with the music driving micro-cuts the instructional logic alone wouldn't justify. Net style: rhythm-forward, overhead-anchored, with front angles used as punctuation — energetic and contemporary rather than classical demo pacing.
